chief_complaint: A 44-year-old man presented with palpitations. He had a recent history of coronavirus disease of 2019 (COVID-19) infection.
history: The patient had a recent history of COVID-19 infection, which may have exacerbated undiagnosed hyperthyroidism, leading to thyroid storm and cardiogenic shock. He was initially improved with V-A ECMO for 4 days, resulting in improved left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF). Despite medical treatment for hyperthyroidism-associated tachycardia, uncontrolled tachycardia persisted. Ten days after weaning V-A ECMO, the patient suddenly developed pulmonary thromboembolism and hypoxia despite ongoing heparinization.
exam: The patient had cardiogenic shock with a left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) of 13%, which improved to 30% after V-A ECMO support. He later developed refractory hypoxemia due to pulmonary thromboembolism.
labs: The patient had a LVEF of 13% initially, which improved to 30% after V-A ECMO support and eventually to 52.5% after thyroidectomy.
